title: To manage 300,000 creators, Unilever automates everything but the relationship
tldr: Unilever scales a 300,000-creator program using automation for logistics but deliberately keeps human relationship-building manual. A concrete blueprint for balancing scale with human-centered creator ops.
why_fresh: concrete case study on human-vs-automation balance at scale
angle: What Unilever's 'automate the logistics, keep the relationship human' model teaches about human-centered content ops.

title: Evergreen Content Is Over – The Individual Is The Only Strategy Left
tldr: Argues the evergreen content playbook is dead and personal, individual-voice content is now the only durable strategy in an AI-search world. A provocative reframe of content strategy fundamentals.
why_fresh: contrarian shift on evergreen/content decay in AI era
angle: Is evergreen really dead, or just repositioned? A content strategist's rebuttal on human voice vs. content decay.

title: Inside the government docuseries that changed minds about animal control
tldr: A county turned ride-alongs with animal control officers into a documentary series that became its most engaging comms strategy. A vivid case study in human-centered, long-form owned content beating short video.
angle: When a docuseries beats a 30-second clip: what public-sector storytelling teaches brands about depth over polish.

title: Publisher ad supply fell by up to 40% in Q2 as AI search choked the open web
tldr: Benchmarking data shows publisher ad supply dropped up to 40% in Q2 2026 as zero-click AI search starves the open web of traffic. A structural shift with big implications for content distribution and media business models.
why_fresh: new hard data on zero-click AI search impact
angle: The open web is quietly starving—what a 40% ad-supply drop means for how content strategists plan distribution.

title: Reddit questions if AI data deals could hurt its ad business
tldr: Reddit is debating whether selling community conversations to AI companies undermines the very ad value those conversations create. A sharp tension at the heart of the community-content business model.
why_fresh: emerging tension in AI data licensing vs. community value
angle: Selling your community to AI: the strategic paradox of monetizing the conversations that fuel your platform.

title: How Die Zeit built a searchable database of Nazi Party members
tldr: Die Zeit turned a surviving Nazi membership card index into a searchable public database, name by name. A masterclass in data journalism, information design, and archival storytelling with civic weight.
angle: One name at a time: how Die Zeit's archive project models information design that carries moral weight.

title: How Dollar Shave Club uses generative AI to unlock advertising creativity
tldr: DSC's brand chief explains how the company uses generative AI not to cut corners but to reassert a distinctive brand voice. A rare concrete case on AI serving voice rather than flattening it.
why_fresh: case study on AI reinforcing brand voice
angle: Using AI to sound more like yourself, not less: DSC's playbook for voice-first generative content.

title: Tinder's first rebrand in nearly a decade channels the voice of a dating columnist
tldr: Porto Rocha rebranded Tinder around a distinct editorial voice and 'designing for contradiction,' giving the app a point of view. A useful case in brand voice and narrative identity for digital products.
why_fresh: fresh rebrand case study on voice-driven design
angle: Giving a product a point of view: what Tinder's editorial-voice rebrand teaches about brand narrative.

title: How to Train AI to Think Like You
tldr: A step-by-step framework for turning your meeting transcripts into a personalized AI profile that replicates how you write and reason. Directly useful for scaling a personal or executive voice.
why_fresh: practical new method for voice-cloning your reasoning
angle: Cloning your own thinking: a hands-on method for scaling executive voice without losing humanity.

title: AI is doing the work. Are your leaders still doing the thinking?
tldr: As AI becomes an actor that initiates and executes decisions, the judgment behind polished output belongs to no one. A sharp warning for leadership and content governance.
why_fresh: new angle on AI eroding decision ownership
angle: When AI produces the output but owns none of the judgment: the leadership accountability gap in automated content.

title: Turning Workers Into Influencers? Key Employment Issues for Employers Before Starting
tldr: A legal-lens look at the risks and requirements when companies turn employees into influencers/creators. Essential grounding for anyone building an employee-generated content program.
why_fresh: underexplored governance angle on employee-generated content
angle: Before you turn staff into creators: the governance and consent scaffolding EGC programs need.

title: Crayola's Adult Pivot Puts Its Brand Story To The Test
tldr: Crayola is stretching its childhood-nostalgia brand toward adult audiences, testing how far a brand narrative can flex. A live case study in repositioning without breaking core identity.
why_fresh: routine brand-pivot story but useful case
angle: How far can a nostalgia brand stretch? Crayola's adult pivot as a narrative elasticity test.

title: Nexxen Identifies 'News-Avoiding Voters' as Key 2026 Midterm Audience
tldr: New research segments 'news-avoiding voters' as a key audience, spotlighting how media-avoidance is reshaping targeting. A useful lens on shifting media-consumption habits.
why_fresh: new audience segment around news avoidance
angle: The rise of 'news-avoiders': what media-avoidance behavior means for how content reaches disengaged audiences.

title: LinkedIn Users Say One 'Sympathy' Reaction Fills Their Feeds With Death
tldr: Users report that LinkedIn's 'sympathy' reaction is skewing feeds toward death and grief content, exposing how a single UI signal reshapes consumption. A telling look at platform mechanics and media habits.
why_fresh: quirky insight into how one signal warps feeds
angle: How a single reaction button reshapes what we consume: LinkedIn's 'sympathy' spiral and feed psychology.
